meta.setting: A cold fence-line camp outside French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in camp life and rootless travel

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional mining roads, settlement movement, and work pressure.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park

image_prompt.subject: two saddle tramps sharing bitter coffee beside a fence at dawn, bedrolls tied high and horses half-asleep behind them
image_prompt.scene: frosted grass outside French Gulch, low fog, distant town smoke, patched coats, a tin cup passed without ceremony, a coil of rope used as a pillow, and no camp comfort beyond what fits in a roll
image_prompt.composition: square intimate camp vignette with negative space around the figures to show rootlessness; horses and fence form the only boundary they can claim
image_prompt.camera: low fire-level view through thin smoke, early-HD character silhouettes and tactile camp objects
material_cues: tin cup rims dulled by smoke
cold ash and one stubborn coal
worn boot leather cracked at the toe
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the scene tender only by accident: nobody is performing hardship, they are just used to it. The coffee should look worse than the weather.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine camp scene
render_traits: slightly simplified figures and horse silhouettes
modest texture resolution on canvas, wool, frost, leather, and smoke
hard ambient occlusion under bedrolls, boots, and fence rails
cold dawn light with one weak ember
believable 1899 clothing, tack, and road camp objects
quiet survival mood rather than heroic composition
style_modifiers.palette: frost gray, ash black, saddle brown, faded wool blue, weak ember orange, fog white, and distant pine green.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable marks, labels, brands, or text. Any paper scrap should be indistinct.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The riders, horses, bedrolls, coffee, fence, and cold camp are fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 road life.
The image shows precarious camp survival without making poverty decorative.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
